steps.title: Find the explicit factual claim about the museum's collection
steps.content: The passage states directly: “has more than 8,500 objects in its collection.” This is a clear factual assertion about the museum itself.

steps.title: Match the statement to the evidence
steps.content:
Choice B says the collection “includes over 8,500 objects” — which is precisely what “more than 8,500” means.

Choices A and C are directly contradicted by the text (the museum “is located” in New York City now, and was founded “in 1969,” not after 2000), and choice D makes a comparative claim about visitor numbers that the text never addresses.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
